summary: Full-stack developer and automation engineer with hands-on experience building production web and desktop applications, async data pipelines, and self-hosted infrastructure. Combines a background in technical problem solving with a passion for identifying and automating manual processes to drive operational efficiency. Currently focused on backend development and process automation.

experience.companyDescription: Socket Fiber is a leading telecommunications provider in Missouri, offering fiber-optic internet, DSL, and home phone services to residential and business customers, known for its high-speed broadband and community-focused operations.
experience.description: Quickly rose through multiple roles in under two years at a rapidly growing fiber ISP, driving operational automation and team leadership through a cost-first approach

roles.title: Operations Automation Engineer
highlights: Automated technician management and reporting processes using Python, reducing manual work by 50+ hours per week
Integrated with custom legacy OSS/BSS software to streamline routing and automate assignment of installation and repair tickets based on technician availability, location, and skill set
Developed dashboard tools for real-time monitoring of automation processes and job statistics, improving visibility and enabling data-driven decision making
Built a multi-user web platform (CalendarBuddy) wrapping three desktop automation tools into a unified portal with Celery + Redis task queue and SSE-based real-time progress streaming
Engineered a Python scraping engine using Playwright to automate browser sessions and extract fiber installation job data, orchestrating 8-16 concurrent browser workers with async task management

roles.title: Senior Residential Dispatcher
highlights: Hand-selected to build out the Technical Operations team, training 6 new Dispatchers and establishing dispatch procedures
Managed scheduling and dispatches for 50+ technicians across 8 contracting companies, averaging 150 daily jobs
Implemented tools to optimize route planning, achieving a 93% job completion rate while reducing average job costs
Implemented assignment tracking procedures, eliminating missed appointments and improving technician accountability
Developed a VRPTW route optimizer with self-hosted geocoding (Nominatim) and routing (OSRM) backends

roles.title: Technical Support Tier II
highlights: Led a team of ~15 support technicians, reducing average ticket lifespan to 48 hours through targeted training and process improvements
Coordinated with ILECs and third-party vendors to resolve complex technical issues and escalations in a timely manner
Maintained an NPS score of 87% through quality-focused support processes and team accountability

experience.companyDescription: Geek Squad is a leading tech support service provider, specializing in installation, maintenance, and repair of consumer electronics and appliances.
experience.description: Progressed from in-store consultation to hands-on automotive technology installation, combining customer-facing sales expertise with technical installation skills

roles.title: AutoTech
highlights: Installed car electronics including stereos, speakers, amplifiers, remote starts, dash cameras, and GPS systems
Diagnosed and resolved complex vehicle electrical issues, ensuring quality installations across a wide range of makes and models
Maintained a high customer satisfaction rating through attention to detail and clean, professional installations
Trained new installers on proper techniques, wiring standards, and safety procedures

roles.title: Consultation Agent
highlights: Provided expert product consultation to customers on home theater, networking, smart home, and car electronics solutions
Assessed customer needs and designed tailored technology solutions, driving attachment rates and repeat business
Collaborated with installation teams to ensure seamless handoffs from sales to service delivery
Consistently exceeded monthly revenue targets through needs-based selling and relationship building

education.description: Focused on programming, networking, and systems administration fundamentals.
2/3 completed. Transferred to University of Missouri in Fall 2022.

pdfProjects.name: CalendarBuddy
highlights: A multi-user web platform that wraps three desktop automation tools — JobScraper, Spreader, and ASSigner — into a unified, automated portal with Celery + Redis task queue, SSE-based real-time progress streaming, and a calendar dashboard.
Built with FastAPI, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL, and Docker. Multi-user authentication with Argon2 hashing, Excel/TXT export, and admin dashboard.
pdfProjects.tech: Python · FastAPI · Celery · Redis · PostgreSQL · Docker

pdfProjects.name: JobScraper
highlights: A Python scraping engine using Playwright to automate browser sessions and extract fiber installation job data. Orchestrates 8-16 concurrent browser workers with async task management and feeds results into a VRPTW route optimizer.
795+ automated tests. Self-hosted geocoding (Nominatim) and routing (OSRM) backends with strategy pattern for swappable providers.
pdfProjects.tech: Python · Playwright · Pandas · VROOM · OSRM · Nominatim
